header.headline: Flash Attention
header.subtitle: Exact attention stays exact by tiling memory pressure down to a streaming problem.

left.title: Old path
left.body: Materialize the full score matrix and memory explodes with sequence length.

right.title: Flash path
right.body: Stream tiles, keep one working set in fast memory, and preserve exactness.

panels.title: Memory wall
panels.summary: Long context attention is often bounded by matrix materialization, not arithmetic.
bullets: Speedups do not help if it cannot fit

panels.title: Tile loop
panels.summary: Process Q against streamed K/V tiles and update partial normalization in place.
panels.note: The loop is structured, not approximate.

panels.title: Exact result
panels.summary: The algorithm changes memory movement while preserving the mathematical answer.

callout.title: Key takeaway
points: Treat attention as a memory problem first.
Tile the computation before asking for more hardware.
Compiler-visible structure beats raw SVG trivia.
